scope.summary: Author tack.wgsl (ball-joint cloth-rigid XPBD coupling per TR-4) and trim_contact.wgsl (one-sided trim-mesh-vs-cloth inequality constraint per TR-5) WGSL compute shaders, wire their bind groups and dispatch into the 8-pass substep sequence at the positions mandated by TR-3.1, and add WGSL/Rust binding validation per the pattern in MT-033
acceptance_criteria: tack.wgsl implements the Muller-Macklin XPBD rigid body coupling formulation (SCA 2020 CGF 39(8)): effective inverse mass includes both translational and rotational contributions per TR-4.1
tack.wgsl is color-partitioned such that no particle index and no trim body index appear in more than one color per dispatch per TR-4.4
tack_lambdas are reset to zero at substep start per TR-4.5
trim_contact.wgsl applies a one-sided inequality constraint (max(0,-C)) pushing cloth particles out of trim triangles within collision_thickness_mm per TR-5.1
For trim meshes with >= 200 triangles, an AABB/BVH pre-filter is used before per-triangle query per TR-5.3
BVH rebuild is forced when body displacement between substeps exceeds 0.5 * collision_thickness_mm per TR-5.4
trim_contact.wgsl writes corrections only to cloth particles, not to GpuTrimBody entries, per TR-5.5
Both shaders appear at the exact substep positions specified in TR-3.1 (tack after bend, trim_contact after tack)
wgsl_to_wgpu or equivalent compile-time binding validation fails the build on struct/layout drift per MT-033 pattern
scope.risk_if_missed: MOAT-5 (mixed cloth-rigid simulation) is spec-mandated and is the primary architectural differentiator for rigid trim physics; without tack.wgsl and trim_contact.wgsl the trim rigid bodies are decoupled from the WGSL substep loop and the XPBD substep order (TR-3.1) is violated; MT-190 integration test (zip jacket + fasten buttons) cannot pass

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
